summary: A somber, archival-grade botanical study where vibrant life meets inevitable decomposition, preserved under layers of translucent, fractured lacquer.
material_cues: glossy varnish overlay with heavy crackle
composition_principles: negative space utilization for dust motes

route_a.objective: Establish a seamless botanical tapestry that flows longitudinally across the weapon geometry.
constraints: no hero flowers crossing component boundaries

route_b.objective: A curated, painterly arrangement where clusters of decay are mapped to unique weapon sections.
composition_rules: weighted focus on the receiver panel

component_roles.stock: High-density saturated peony blooms integrated with light craquelure.
component_roles.receiver: Central heroic composition featuring overlapping floral elements and brass filigree.
component_roles.magazine: Secondary flow of barbed tendrils and skeleton leaves curving downward.
component_roles.pistol_grip: Single decaying leaf motif providing grounded transition.
component_roles.handguard: Thinned vine progression acting as a visual bridge.
component_roles.front_assembly: Soft ink wash stains fading into monochromatic tones.
component_roles.barrel_muzzle: Quiet atmosphere of isolated dust motes and subtle parchment grain.

candidate_directions.title: Archival Sepia Study
candidate_directions.concept: Focus on the monochromatic, dried-out state of a historical herbarium.

candidate_directions.title: Varnished Bloom
candidate_directions.concept: Emphasizes the glossy, cracked surface layer trapping fresh flora beneath.

candidate_directions.title: The Barbed Path
candidate_directions.concept: Prioritizes the wild, aggressive growth of vines wrapping the mechanical structure.

candidate_directions.title: Decay Gradient
candidate_directions.concept: Visual metaphor for time: vibrant flora on stock, decaying to dust at muzzle.
candidate_directions.route_a_emphasis: Seamless tonal transition from deep burgundy to ash

evaluation_criteria: Floral elements must feel organic and hand-illustrated.
Contrast between fresh/aged materials must be clear.
Detail density must decline significantly from stock to barrel.
reference_policy: Adhere to historical 18th-century botanical sketches; no modern digital art references.
